title: Smart Contract Hardening: Upgradeability, Governance Roles, and Security Review
description: Harden all four registry contracts with transparent proxy upgradeability pattern (OpenZeppelin TransparentUpgradeableProxy), establish a multi-sig governance model using OpenZeppelin Governor or TimelockController for admin role management, and perform a structured security review checklist. This ensures contracts can be patched post-deployment without losing state.
objectives: All contracts upgradeable without data loss using transparent proxy pattern
TimelockController with minimum 48-hour delay for sensitive admin operations
Documented upgrade procedure with scripted safeguards
Security review checklist completed and signed off

steps: Step 1 - Upgrade Pattern Selection: Use OpenZeppelin TransparentUpgradeableProxy. Implementation contracts (IssuerRegistryV1, etc.) inherit Initializable instead of using constructors. Storage layout must be preserved across upgrades — document in NatSpec comments.

Step 2 - Initializable Migration: Remove constructors from all registry contracts. Replace with initialize(address initialAdmin) function decorated with initializer modifier (OpenZeppelin Initializable). All state initialization moves into initialize().
Step 3 - ProxyAdmin Contract: Deploy ProxyAdmin (OZ v5). ProxyAdmin is owned by TimelockController. Never allow EOA to directly control ProxyAdmin — all upgrades must go through governance.

Step 4 - TimelockController Setup: Deploy TimelockController with minimumDelay=48 hours (172800 seconds) for mainnet, 300 seconds for testnet. proposers: [governance multisig address]. executors: [address(0)] (anyone can execute after delay). admin: address(0) after setup (no direct admin).

Step 5 - Upgrade Script (scripts/upgrade/upgrade-v2.ts): Uses hardhat-upgrades plugin (@openzeppelin/hardhat-upgrades). Steps: (1) deploy new implementation, (2) prepare upgrade calldata, (3) propose to TimelockController with required delay, (4) execute after delay. Store upgrade history in deployments/{network}/upgrades.json.

Step 6 - Storage Layout Documentation: In each contract, add @custom:storage-layout NatSpec block listing all storage slot assignments. When adding a new variable in V2+, always append — never reorder or remove. Use __gap[50] as reserved storage slots at end of each contract.
Step 7 - Reentrancy Guard: All state-mutating external functions in registry contracts that do external calls (cross-contract to IssuerRegistry) must use ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant modifier).

Step 8 - Security Checklist: (1) Integer overflow: all contracts use ^0.8.25 (built-in overflow protection). (2) Access control: all mutating functions have onlyRole. (3) Reentrancy: covered by ReentrancyGuard. (4) Denial of service: array iteration capped at 1000. (5) Uninitialized proxy: initializer modifier prevents double init. (6) Front-running: not applicable (registry operations are admin-only). (7) Event monitoring: all state changes emit events. (8) Emergency pause: all registries pausable.

Step 9 - Deployment Procedure Documentation: Create docs/contracts/upgrade-procedure.md. Section 1: Pre-upgrade (run npx hardhat-upgrades validate to check storage layout compatibility). Section 2: Deploy new implementation. Section 3: Propose upgrade to Timelock. Section 4: Wait for delay. Section 5: Execute upgrade. Section 6: Smoke test on-chain.

Step 10 - Contract Size Check: All contracts must be < 24KB (EIP-170 limit). Run npx hardhat size-contracts after each modification. If approaching limit, extract library functions to external libraries.
security_constraints: initializer modifier MUST be present on all initialize() functions — double initialization is a critical bug
__gap[50] reserved storage slots MUST be present in all upgradeable contracts
ProxyAdmin MUST be owned by TimelockController — direct EOA ownership is prohibited
minimumDelay MUST be >= 172800 seconds (48h) on mainnet chains
Storage layout compatibility MUST be validated before every upgrade via hardhat-upgrades validate
Upgrade proposals MUST be submitted with human-readable description in Timelock calldata

acceptance_criteria: All four contracts deploy as transparent proxies without errors
npx hardhat-upgrades validate passes for all contracts
Double-calling initialize() reverts with InvalidInitialization error
Upgrade via TimelockController succeeds after delay
Direct call to ProxyAdmin without going through Timelock reverts
